2605.03936.title: The Counterexample Game: Iterated Conceptual Analysis and Repair in Language Models
2605.03936.paper_summary: Conceptual analysis -- proposing definitions and refining them through counterexamples -- is central to philosophical methodology. We study whether language models can perform this task through iterated analysis and repair chains: one model instance generates counterexamples to a proposed definition, another repairs the definition, and the process repeats. Across 20 concepts and thousands of counterexample-repair cycles, we find that, although many LM-generated counterexamples are judged invalid by both expert humans and an LM judge, the LM judge accepts roughly twice as many as humans do. Nonetheless, per-item validity judgments are moderately consistent across humans and between humans and the LM. We further find that extended iteration produces increasingly verbose definitions without improving accuracy. We also see that some concepts resist stable definitions in general. These findings suggest that while LMs can engage in philosophical reasoning, the counterexample-repair loop hits diminishing returns quickly and could be a fruitful test case for evaluating whether LMs can sustain high-level iterated philosophical reasoning.
2605.03936.paper_summary_zh: 概念分析——通過反例提出定義並加以完善——是哲學方法論的核心。我們研究語言模型是否能通過反覆分析和修正鏈來執行這一任務：一個模型實例生成對提議定義的反例，另一個修正該定義，然後過程重複。在20個概念和數千次反例-修正循環中，我們發現，儘管許多由LM生成的反例被專家人類和LM評判者判定為無效，但LM評判者接受的反例大約是人類的兩倍。儘管如此，對每個項目的有效性判斷在人類之間以及人類與LM之間是中等一致的。我們進一步發現，延長迭代會產生越來越冗長的定義，但並未提高準確性。我們還看到某些概念在一般情況下抵抗穩定定義。這些發現表明，儘管LM可以參與哲學推理，但反例-修正循環很快會遇到收益遞減的情況，並可能成為評估LM是否能持續高水平反覆哲學推理的有益測試案例。
2605.03936.author: Daniel Drucker et.al.
